title: lupinus argenteus subsp. rubricaulis
intro: Lupinus argenteus subsp. rubricaulis, also known as the redstem lupine, is a perennial herb belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae) and native to the western United States and Canada. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of redstem lupine include its potential to treat various ailments, such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The herbal preparations of redstem lupine typically involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or internally, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
